key_technical_questions.heading: Key Technical Questions
bullets: Sandbox provisioning for Integration Cloud — Director of Certification or Director of Partner Enablement, Customer Education team. Does Workday offer NFR or training-specific tenant instances for Integration Cloud, and if so, can they be provisioned and torn down programmatically via API, or are they manually requested through a partner/customer support process?

Integration Cloud API surface for state validation — Principal Engineer or Technical Lead on the Integration Cloud platform team. Can the Workday REST API (or SOAP/RAAS) be used to programmatically verify the state of a completed integration build — for example, confirming that a specific integration system, transformation, or connector step exists and is correctly configured — so we can auto-score learner work in a simulated or sandboxed environment?

Multi-tenancy and identity model for lab users — Director of Partner Enablement or Solution Architect, Customer Education. When provisioning hands-on lab environments for partners or customers learning Integration Cloud, does each learner need a unique Workday tenant with a dedicated security group, or can multiple concurrent learners share a single tenant with role-based isolation — and what identity provider constraints (Okta, SAML federation) apply to temporary lab user accounts?

conversation_starters.heading: Conversation Starters
bullets: Workday Integration Cloud has so many connection points—payroll systems, benefits platforms, financial apps—that when someone misconfigures a mapping or doesn't understand the data flow, it can break payroll or create compliance issues across the whole ecosystem. Hands-on labs let your customers and partners actually build and test integrations in a safe environment before they touch production.

A lot of integration failures happen because people understand the *concept* but haven't actually seen how Workday's transformation logic works with their specific legacy system. When your team can practice real scenarios—like handling date format mismatches or null value rules—they catch problems during training instead of at go-live.

Your partners are selling Workday implementations to enterprises with thousands of employees. If they can't confidently demonstrate they know how to set up integrations, you lose deals. Certification through hands-on labs gives them—and your customers—proof they can actually execute, not just pass a multiple-choice test.

account_intelligence.heading: Account Intelligence
bullets: Workday operates a partner enablement program requiring certification training at scale — their Director of Certification/Partner Enablement role signals structured partner onboarding needs

As a SaaS-only ERP platform, Workday likely trains customers on complex cloud workflows — hands-on labs could differentiate their customer education vs. competitors like SAP SuccessFactors and Oracle HCM

products.name: Workday Extend (Platform Extensibility)
products.subcategory: Low-Code App Development on Enterprise Cloud
lab_concepts: Build Your First Workday Extend App
Design an Extend Solution from Requirements
Configure Extend App Security and Permissions
recommendation: Simulation is the only viable delivery path for Workday Extend — the product runs exclusively on Workday's proprietary SaaS cloud with no self-hosted, Azure, AWS, or container deployment option, and no programmatic per-learner tenant provisioning API. Simulation labs can teach Extend Studio workflows, business object design, and deployment patterns without requiring a live Workday tenant. This mirrors how Workday Education currently delivers conceptual training — the gap is hands-on practice, which simulation partially addresses.

Scoring is limited to simulation-guided interaction checkpoints and MCQ knowledge validation. AI Vision can evaluate Extend Studio screen state for UI-based task completion. No programmatic API scoring is available without a live tenant. Certification readiness programs should use MCQ to validate conceptual understanding of Extend architecture and deployment patterns.

Workday's proprietary cloud architecture and locked provisioning APIs are a fundamental, unresolved blocker — identical to the constraint that makes Workday HCM a canonical poor match in Skillable's benchmarks. The Skillable team invested over a year with engaged Workday training leadership but the product team has not opened APIs. Workday Extend inherits this constraint entirely. Do not invest significant solution engineering time until Workday commits to a programmatic sandbox API for Extend developer tenants.

The specific question that unblocks this engagement is whether Workday's product team will open a programmatic sandbox API for per-learner Extend developer tenants. The Workday Education or Partner Enablement team owns the training relationship, but the Workday Platform Product team owns the API decision. Your champion should ask: 'Does Workday have a roadmap item for programmatically provisioned Extend developer sandboxes via API — similar to how Salesforce Trailhead provisions Developer Edition orgs — that would enable automated per-learner lab environments for training partners?'

If Workday opens a developer sandbox API in the future, the highest-value entry motion is partner technical enablement — Workday's SI and ISV partner ecosystem needs Extend competency to win and deliver extensibility engagements. The Commvault expansion arc (SE enablement → customer training → partner training → certification) is the right model: start with partner developer labs, then expand to customer admin training, then embed in Workday Pro certification as a performance-based component. Until the API blocker is resolved, maintain the relationship at low cost and monitor Workday's developer platform roadmap.

dimensions.name: Provisioning
dimensions.summary: Workday Extend is SaaS-only on Workday's proprietary cloud. No self-hosted option, no Azure/AWS path, no Docker image. Workday developer tenants exist for partners but are provisioned manually by Workday — not via API. Simulation is the only viable delivery path. Provisioning score reflects simulation tier with severe constraints on automation.

evidence.claim: **Delivery Method | Context:** Workday Extend is SaaS-only on Workday's proprietary cloud — simulation is the only viable lab delivery path because no self-hosted, Azure, AWS, or container deployment option exists for the Workday platform. Simulation labs can teach Extend Studio concepts and app design patterns without a live tenant.

**No Self-Hosted Option | Blocker:** Workday Extend has no installable, containerized, or cloud-marketplace deployment path — it runs exclusively inside Workday's proprietary SaaS cloud. This is the same architectural constraint that makes Workday HCM a canonical poor match for hands-on lab delivery.

**Developer Tenant Access | Risk:** Workday offers developer tenants for Extend through its partner program, but these are provisioned manually by Workday — not via API — and take significant time and cost to set up. No self-service or programmatic per-learner provisioning path exists, making scale delivery infeasible.

dimensions.name: Lab Access
dimensions.summary: No per-learner isolation mechanism exists. Workday developer tenants are shared, manually provisioned, and not recyclable between learners at scale. No programmatic account creation API. Lab access is fundamentally constrained by Workday's closed SaaS architecture.

evidence.claim: **Closed API Architecture | Blocker:** Workday's platform APIs are locked down and not available for automated per-learner tenant provisioning. No programmatic account creation, license application, or environment configuration is possible at lab scale — a fundamental constraint of Workday's SaaS architecture.

**Tenant Cost | Risk:** Workday tenant provisioning costs thousands of dollars and takes months — making per-learner isolated environments economically and operationally infeasible for any training program at scale. This cost structure eliminates credential pool and trial account paths.

dimensions.name: Scoring
dimensions.summary: Simulation scoring via guided interaction is the primary method. AI Vision can evaluate Extend Studio screen state for UI-based tasks. MCQ knowledge checks cover app design concepts and deployment patterns. No programmatic API scoring available without a live tenant.

evidence.claim: **Simulation Scoring | Context:** In a simulation environment, guided interaction scoring can validate learner decisions at key workflow steps — app design choices, business object configuration, deployment sequencing. This is the highest-fidelity scoring method available given the absence of a live Workday tenant.

**Knowledge Check Scoring | Context:** MCQ-based assessment can validate conceptual understanding of Workday Extend architecture, business object design patterns, and deployment lifecycle — appropriate for certification readiness programs where live tenant scoring is unavailable.

dimensions.name: Teardown
dimensions.summary: Simulation environments have no persistent state to clean up — teardown is automatic. No orphan risk in a simulation context. Full marks for teardown given the simulation delivery path.

evidence.claim: **Simulation Teardown | Strength:** Simulation environments carry no persistent state — teardown is automatic and instantaneous. No orphaned tenants, accounts, or data to clean up between learner sessions. This is the one dimension where the simulation path has a clear advantage over real-tenant delivery.

dimensions.name: Product Complexity
dimensions.summary: Workday Extend requires developers to learn a proprietary development model: Extend Studio IDE, business object design, custom REST API creation, orchestration workflows, UI framework, security model, and deployment lifecycle. Multiple distinct phases from design through deployment. Integration with Workday's data model adds significant complexity. Role breadth spans developer, admin, and architect. Not learnable by watching — requires iterative hands-on practice.

evidence.claim: **Extend Studio Complexity | Strength:** Workday Extend requires developers to master a proprietary IDE (Extend Studio), business object design, custom REST API creation, orchestration workflows, a Workday-specific UI framework, and a non-standard security model — a multi-phase learning journey that cannot be completed by watching documentation or videos.
evidence.source_title: Workday Technology Platform — Extensibility and Interoperability

evidence.claim: **App Lifecycle Phases | Strength:** Workday Extend development spans distinct phases — design (business object modeling), build (Extend Studio coding), configure (security and permissions), deploy (tenant deployment), and maintain (monitoring and updates) — each requiring separate skill development and hands-on practice.

**Workday Data Model Integration | Strength:** Extend apps must integrate deeply with Workday's proprietary data model, business process framework, and security architecture — creating significant integration complexity that requires hands-on practice to master, not just conceptual understanding.
evidence.source_title: Workday Technology Platform — Extensibility and Interoperability

dimensions.name: Mastery Stakes
dimensions.summary: Misconfigured Extend apps can break core Workday HCM and Finance business processes, cause data integrity failures, or create compliance exposure in regulated industries. Workday's proprietary development model has a steep learning curve — the platform is non-standard and requires significant investment to reach competency. Partner adoption of Extend is a documented challenge, with many partners struggling to build production-quality extensions.

evidence.claim: **Business Process Risk | Strength:** Misconfigured Workday Extend apps can break core HCM and Finance business processes — payroll errors, compliance failures, data integrity issues — in production Workday tenants used by thousands of employees. The consequence of poor Extend development is direct operational and regulatory risk.

**Proprietary Development Model | Strength:** Workday Extend uses a non-standard, Workday-specific development paradigm — developers with strong general programming skills still face a significant learning curve mastering Extend Studio, Workday's data model, and the platform's security and deployment model.

**Partner Adoption Challenge | Strength:** Workday's Talent Development Partner program (ASU, Marymount) and extensive Workday Pro certification curriculum signal that partner and customer adoption of Extend is a documented challenge requiring structured training investment — a direct indicator of hands-on lab demand.

dimensions.name: Lab Versatility
dimensions.summary: Break/Fix scenarios are highly applicable — debugging broken Extend apps with misconfigured business objects or failed orchestrations is a realistic and high-value lab type. Architecture Challenge labs (design and build a custom Extend solution from requirements) map directly to real partner engagement scenarios.

evidence.claim: **Broken Extend App Scenarios | Opportunity:** Debugging a misconfigured Workday Extend app — broken business object relationships, failed orchestration steps, security permission errors — is a realistic and high-value lab scenario that maps directly to real partner engagement challenges.

**Design-and-Build Scenarios | Opportunity:** Architecture Challenge labs — where learners receive a customer requirement and must design and build a complete Extend solution — mirror real partner pre-sales and delivery scenarios, making them high-value for partner technical enablement programs.
evidence.source_title: Workday Technology Platform — Extensibility and Interoperability

dimensions.name: Market Demand
dimensions.summary: ERP/CRM category (moderate demand) plus App Development secondary category. Workday has ~10,000+ enterprise customers globally, creating a large population of potential Extend developers and admins. Active Workday Pro certification program. AI-embedded features (Sana AI) accessible via Extend add hands-on practice demand. Growing enterprise platform extensibility category.

evidence.claim: **Enterprise Install Base | Strength:** Workday serves over 10,000 enterprise customers globally across HCM and Finance — each a potential Extend developer and admin training population. The scale of the install base creates significant latent demand for Extend training even at low adoption rates.

**Workday Pro Certification | Strength:** Workday Pro Platform Administrator Certification is an active, structured certification program with formal exams — confirming a credentialing ecosystem that creates demand for hands-on practice and assessment, even if the provisioning constraints limit real-lab delivery today.

**Sana AI Integration | Opportunity:** Workday's Sana AI platform and embedded AI agents (Deployment Agent, BP Optimize Agent) are accessible via Extend — creating demand for hands-on practice on AI-powered extensibility features that cannot be learned through documentation alone.

dimensions.name: Training Commitment
dimensions.summary: Workday Education is a mature, well-resourced training organization with courses for customers, partners, and the general public. Active Workday Pro certification program with multiple tracks. Talent Development Partners (ASU W.P. Carey, Marymount University) confirm formal ATP-equivalent network. Broad training catalog spanning AI, admin, configuration, and user training. Training permeates the business across multiple teams.

evidence.claim: **Workday Education Catalog | Strength:** Workday Education offers a broad training catalog spanning AI fundamentals, Workday Basics, product configuration, admin certification, and user training — available to customers, partners, and the public. The catalog depth confirms a mature, well-resourced training organization.

**Workday Pro Certification | Strength:** Workday Pro Platform Administrator Certification is an active program with formal exams for customers and partners — confirming organizational commitment to credentialing and a structured pathway that hands-on labs could directly support.

**Talent Development Partners | Strength:** Workday has formalized Talent Development Partnerships with ASU W.P. Carey School of Business and Marymount University to deliver Workday Pro certification programs globally — confirming a structured channel enablement model beyond internal training.

dimensions.name: Organizational Dna
dimensions.summary: Workday has a large and active partner ecosystem of SIs, ISVs, and implementation partners — Extend is specifically designed to enable this ecosystem. Integration Maturity is high for Extend itself (the product IS an extensibility platform). Workday is a large enterprise organization — accessible but requires navigating a complex org structure. Historically builds training in-house but uses external Talent Development Partners for certification delivery.

evidence.claim: **SI and ISV Partner Network | Strength:** Workday has a large ecosystem of system integrator and ISV partners who implement and extend Workday for enterprise customers — Extend is specifically designed to enable this partner ecosystem, creating a large population of partner developers who need Extend training.
evidence.source_title: Workday Technology Platform — Extensibility and Interoperability

evidence.claim: **Open Extensibility Platform | Strength:** Workday Extend IS an extensibility and interoperability platform — the product itself is designed for integration and custom development, confirming high integration maturity and an API-forward philosophy for the extensibility layer (even if the core SaaS APIs are locked for provisioning).

**Large Enterprise Complexity | Risk:** Workday is a large, complex enterprise organization. The benchmark explicitly flags a pattern of engaged training leadership but a product team unwilling to open APIs — navigating this org requires executive sponsorship and patience. Relationship investment should be calibrated to the low product labability score.

dimensions.name: Delivery Capacity
dimensions.summary: Talent Development Partners (ASU, Marymount) confirmed for Workday Pro certification delivery. Extensive on-demand and instructor-led catalog via Workday Education. No confirmed Skillable or competitor lab platform in use. Third-party Workday training market exists (gray market signal). Strong delivery infrastructure but no confirmed hands-on lab delivery channel.

evidence.claim: **University Delivery Partners | Strength:** ASU W.P. Carey and Marymount University are confirmed Talent Development Partners delivering Workday Pro certification programs globally — a structured ATP-equivalent network that could serve as a distribution channel for simulation-based Extend training.

**Third-Party Workday Training | Context:** A significant third-party training market exists for Workday skills (Udemy, LinkedIn Learning, independent consultants) — confirming market demand for Workday Extend training even outside official Workday Education channels.

dimensions.name: Build Capacity
dimensions.summary: Workday Education is a dedicated training organization with instructional designers and content developers. Scale of training catalog indicates a capable content development function. No confirmed hands-on lab authoring capability — Workday's training appears primarily video and documentation-based. Professional Services opportunity if Workday pursues simulation lab development.

evidence.claim: **Workday Education Team | Strength:** The scale and breadth of Workday Education's catalog — spanning AI, admin, configuration, user training, and certification — confirms a dedicated content development organization with instructional designers and technical writers capable of building simulation lab content.

**University Partner Content | Context:** Workday's use of university Talent Development Partners for certification delivery suggests a hybrid build model — some content built in-house, some delivered through external partners. Simulation lab authoring would likely require Skillable Professional Services support given the novel format.

ceilings_applied.reason: Pure SaaS with no per-learner sandbox API
No API automation feasible — manual provisioning only

key_technical_questions.heading: Key Technical Questions
bullets: Sandbox provisioning API — Find a Principal Engineer or Staff Engineer on the Workday Extend Platform team. Does Workday Extend expose an API (REST or otherwise) to programmatically provision and tear down isolated tenant sandboxes or development environments, and if so, what is the typical spin-up time and are there rate limits on create/destroy operations?

NFR / partner dev-tenant licensing — Find the Director of Partner Enablement or ISV Alliances lead in the Partner organization. Can Workday issue not-for-resale or training-purpose Extend dev tenants in bulk (e.g., 50-200 concurrent) for hands-on lab scenarios, and what licensing or contractual vehicle covers that use case today?

State validation surface for scoring — Find a Senior Engineer or Technical Lead on the Extend Developer Experience team. When a learner completes a task inside an Extend app (e.g., creates a custom object, deploys an orchestration, or configures a business process), is there a queryable API or event stream that returns the current object/configuration state so an external system can programmatically verify task completion?

account_intelligence.heading: Account Intelligence
bullets: Workday has dedicated VP-level roles for Customer Education and Partner Enablement — indicating structured, scaled training programs that likely require hands-on lab environments for certification and partner onboarding

As an enterprise software platform company, Workday manages complex product extensibility (Workday Extend) that typically demands technical enablement and certification programs for implementation partners and customers
